column2: for example 1
column5: Chapter Title (Mandatory)
column7: Question ID (Mandatory and Unique, for example: 1.1, 1.2, Etc)
column10: Answer Type (Mandatory, one of the following options: 1 for Open Answer, 2 for Dropdown - Single Select, 3 for Dropdown - Single Select - Open answer, 4 for Date, 5 for Dropdown - Multiple Select, 6 for Dropdown - Multiple Select - Open answer)
column11: Dropdown Options (Mandatory if column G is Dropdown. List options for the dropdown separated by a pipe character, for example: Yes|No|Undefined)
column12: DropDown Warnings (Optional, for every option on column H you can define a message, for example: Great|Oh|Think twice)
column13: Dropdown Scoring (Mandatory, set this to 1 if you want to enable scoring for this question. Otherwise set to 0)
column14: Dropdown Option Score (Mandatory if J is set to 1, set the score of the question as an integer positive number, for example 10)
column15: Dropdown Option Score Multiplier (Mandatory if J is set to 1. For every option of the dropdown in column H define an integer positive number that will be multiplied by the value on column K, for example: 1|2|10)
column16: Dropdown Option Show another Question (Optional and only possible if column G is dropdown.For every option in column H you can define one or more question IDs from column D to be shown when the option is selected. For example: 1.2 1.6||6.7 would display questions 1.2 and 1.6 if the first option is selected, none if the second is selected and 6.7 if the third option is selected)
column17: Question Comments & Attachments (Mandatory, select 0 if you do not want to show this option or 1 if you want to let users provide comments and attachments for each question)

column2: Responsibilities
column3: - Management: Ensures the risk management process is established, resourced, and aligned with business objectives. - The Information Security function: Defines and maintains the risk assessment and treatment methodology and ensures consistency of application, with the support of other department managers. - Risk Owners: Are accountable for risks, approve treatment actions within their authority, and accept residual risks. - Department Managers: Support risk assessment and treatment activities. - All Personnel: Contribute to identifying and reporting risks. ---

column2: Policy Statements
column3: - Information security risks must be assessed using defined likelihood and impact criteria. - Risk levels must be derived using a likelihood × impact matrix. - Risks must be treated according to their classified risk level. - Risk treatment and acceptance must follow defined approval requirements. - All risk decisions must be documented and auditable. ---

column2: Standard - Risk Classification Criteria
column3: #### Likelihood - High (3): Happened before and could happen again. - Medium (2): Never happened before but could happen. - Low (1): Never happened before and is unlikely to happen. #### Impact - High (3): Contractual or legal repercussions. - Medium (2): Reputational repercussions. - Low (1): Negligible impact to customers or the organization. #### Risk Level Determination - Risk level is calculated as Likelihood × Impact. - The resulting risk score is mapped to a risk level using the approved risk matrix. ---
column5: Does this requirement reflect your company current practice: High (3) likelihood means the event happened before and could happen again.

column3: #### Likelihood - High (3): Happened before and could happen again. - Medium (2): Never happened before but could happen. - Low (1): Never happened before and is unlikely to happen. #### Impact - High (3): Contractual or legal repercussions. - Medium (2): Reputational repercussions. - Low (1): Negligible impact to customers or the organization. #### Risk Level Determination - Risk level is calculated as Likelihood × Impact. - The resulting risk score is mapped to a risk level using the approved risk matrix. ---

column2: Standard - Risk Treatment Matrix and Approval Requirements
column3: Risk treatment decisions must follow the risk matrix defined by likelihood and impact, with associated approval requirements: #### Low Risk - Characteristics: - Low overall impact to the organization. - Risk is considered acceptable. - Treatment: - Risk may be accepted or treated at the discretion of the risk owner. - Approval: - Risk Owner approval only. - No additional management approval is required. #### Medium Risk - Characteristics: - Noticeable business or reputational impact. - Risk requires active management. - Treatment: - Risk must be treated using internal controls, policies, exceptions, or projects. - Approval: - Risk Owner - Risk Owner’s Manager - GRC Manager #### High (Serious) Risk - Characteristics: - Significant contractual, legal, or business impact. - Risk is not acceptable without executive awareness. - Treatment: - Risk must be treated and tracked until reduced to an acceptable level or formally accepted. - Approval: - Risk Owner - Risk Owner’s Manager - GRC Manager - Director-level approval ---
column5: Does this requirement reflect your company current practice: Low risks have the following characteristics.

column5: What evidence can you provide to demonstrate this requirement is implemented? Examples may include screenshots, reports, logs, tickets, approvals, procedures, meeting records, or system exports.
